# Fix dialog positioning bug (Tailwind v4)

Parent: [ZAI-10](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-10). **Note: this is NOT a localization regression — the buggy line exists in master too** (verified `git diff master -- ui/src/components/ui/dialog.tsx` is empty). Filing as a small fast fix because the board surfaced it during localization testing.

## Symptom

Reported by board on [ZAI-10](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-10): on `/CMP/issues`, click ""+ New issue"". Dialog appears with:
- Brown/non-transparent background
- Dialog flies off-center (drops below viewport center on md+ screens)

## Root cause (board diagnosis, verified)

`ui/src/components/ui/dialog.tsx:62` className stack:

`
... translate-x-[-50%] translate-y-0 md:translate-y-[-50%] ...
`

Board's commit-message hint: *""fix(dialog): remove redundant translate-y-0 causing md:translate-y-[-50%] override in Tailwind v4""*

In Tailwind v4 the utility-class precedence between base `translate-y-0` and responsive `md:translate-y-[-50%]` doesn't cascade as expected — base wins on md+ and the dialog ends up un-centered.

Structured report (issue document) mapping each finding to file:line, component, and suggested i18n key — ready for Localization Agent to execute.

Stop agents from auto-adding `Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>` (or any `Co-Authored-By:` / "Generated with") trailer to commits in this repo. Parent: [ZAI-67](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-67).

The rule already exists in [`AGENTS.md` §5a](/) but it is being overridden in practice by a contradictory instruction in the bundled paperclip skill, which is loaded into every agent's prompt every heartbeat.

## Required changes

### 1. Fix the contradictory skill instruction (root cause)

File: `skills/paperclip/SKILL.md` — line ~234 currently reads:

> **Commit Co-author**: if you make a git commit you MUST add EXACTLY `Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>` to the end of each commit message. Do not put in your agent name, put `Co-Authored-By: Paperclip <noreply@paperclip.ing>`.

Replace it with an instruction that defers to `AGENTS.md` §5a, e.g.:

> **Commit trailers**: do not add `Co-Authored-By:` (any value, including `Paperclip`, `Claude`, `Anthropic`), "Generated with", or any AI-tool attribution trailer to commit messages. The single human author on the `Author:` line is the only attribution we keep. See `AGENTS.md` §5a "Git Commit Rules".

This is a Markdo

title: i18n: configure missing-key safeguards (no-crash fallback to English)
description:
# Configure i18next missing-key safeguards

Parent: [ZAI-10](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-10). **Not blocked** — this is an i18n config tweak in `ui/src/locales/i18n.ts`, runs in parallel with the design-revert audit ([ZAI-43](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-43)).

## Why

Board reported that when a translation key is missing in a non-English locale, ""the entire system falls."" Currently `i18n.init()` only sets `fallbackLng: ""en""` and `defaultNS: ""common""` (see `ui/src/locales/i18n.ts:295-296`). It does not configure:

- `returnEmptyString` (default `true` — empty string values render as empty UI, can break layouts)
- `returnNull` (default `true` — null values render as ""null"" or crash interpolation)
- `returnObjects` (default `false` — but a malformed JSON entry that happens to be an object crashes React render)
- `parseMissingKeyHandler` / `missingKeyHandler` — no centralized fallback for unknown keys

A partial or malformed translation file in any one of 8 locales currently risks runtime crashes.

## Acceptance

In `ui/src/locales/i18n.ts`, the `i18n.init({...})` call adds:

`	s
fallbackLng: ""en"",
returnEmptyString: false,    // empty string values fall through to fallbackLng
returnNull: false,

title: i18n: add ru/settings.json _other plural variants for backup_retention
description:
## Scope

Add the three **`_other` plural fallback variants** currently missing from `ui/src/locales/ru/settings.json`:

```
instance.backup_retention.days_other
instance.backup_retention.weeks_other
instance.backup_retention.months_other
```

The RU file already has `_one`, `_few`, and `_many` for each. The `_other` key is the i18next fallback plural when no other rule matches — required for completeness even if Russian grammar rarely triggers it.

Reference EN values:
- `days_other`: `"{{count}} days"`
- `weeks_other`: `"{{count}} weeks"`
- `months_other`: `"{{count}} months"`

Existing RU pattern (use same style):
- `days_one`: `"{{count}} день"`, `days_few`: `"{{count}} дня"`, `days_many`: `"{{count}} дней"`

## Acceptance criteria

1. Programmatic parity check returns **0 missing keys** for `ru/settings.json`.
2. Commit message follows [ZAI-21](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-21): no `Co-Authored-By` or AI-tool trailers.
3. Scope-bounded: only add these 3 keys, nothing else.

  `"Inference charge"`, `"Platform fee"`, `"Credit purchase"`, `"Credit refund"`, `"Credit expiry"`, `"BYOK fee"`, `"Gateway overhead"`, `"Log storage"`, `"Logpush"`, `"Provisioned capacity"`, `"Training"`, `"Custom model import"`, `"Custom model storage"`, `"Manual adjustment"`
- `financeDirectionDisplayName()` — 2 hardcoded labels: `"Credit"`, `"Debit"`

These functions have no access to i18next's `t()` function, so they will always render in English regardless of the active locale.

The `FinanceTimelineCard.tsx` badge labels (lines 41 and 43) are the only callers of these two functions.

## Acceptance

- Add `finance_event_kind` and `finance_direction` sections to `costs.json` in all 8 locales (`en`, `de`, `el`, `es`, `pt`, `ru`, `uk`, `zh`)
- Update `FinanceTimelineCard.tsx` (or a helper hook) to call `t("costs:finance_event_kind.inference_charge")` etc.

## Goal

title: Board policy: handle upstream @paperclipai/ refs vs branch-protection rule
description:
# Branch protection blocks upstream `@paperclipai/` refs - needs board policy decision

Surfaced by [CTO](/ZAI/agents/cto) during the manual sync run [ZAI-132](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-132) (parent: [ZAI-77](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-77)).

## The problem

Repo branch-protection rule has pattern `(?i)(co-author|author|???)|@` - case-insensitive match on "co-author", "author", Russian "???", or any `@` symbol. The intent (per [AGENTS.md �5a](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-67#document-agents)) is to block AI co-author trailers and stray @-mentions in commit history.

Upstream `paperclipai/paperclip` PR commit bodies legitimately reference `@paperclipai/<package>` (npm scoped package names, e.g. `@paperclipai/cli`). The rule's `@` clause matches those, so direct upstream-commit pushes are blocked. CTO worked around it via squash commit `4c6c850f`, which is why **0 files are behind upstream** but the GitHub UI **still shows 4 commits behind** - squash workflow + protection rule prevents fast-forward reconciliation.

Functional impact: none today (we are file-level current). Cosmetic + future impact: every routine fire repeats this dance; squash history diverges further from upstream lineage; rebases get harder.

title: i18n: 'PROJECTS' sidebar section header untranslated
description:
## Source

title: Health-monitor routine creates duplicate issues + cancelling them returns HTTP 500
description:
The CEO health-monitor routine (`routineId=c6af9585-a736-4376-80de-c1f7fb7d6afc`) is producing one fresh issue per fire instead of reusing a single recurring issue. Active duplicates as of 2026-05-06 21:38Z: [ZAI-98](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-98), [ZAI-113](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-113), [ZAI-117](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-117), [ZAI-123](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-123).

Attempting to PATCH any of the older three to `status: cancelled` (with or without comment) returns `HTTP 500 Internal server error`. POST /api/issues/{id}/checkout also returns 500. They each have a stale `checkoutRunId` from a prior heartbeat run that never released.

**What to investigate:**

1. Routine config — should `concurrencyPolicy` reuse an existing in-progress issue (or skip-if-in-progress) instead of spawning new ones each fire?

3. Once the bug is fixed (or via direct DB action), force-close ZAI-98/113/117 so they don't keep showing in active inbox.

curl -X PATCH -H "Authorization: Bearer $PAPERCLIP_API_KEY" -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d '{"status":"cancelled"}' $PAPERCLIP_API_URL/

title: i18n: Keep onboarding task seed prompts in English regardless of UI locale

Board request from screenshot on [ZAI-26](/ZAI/issues/ZAI-26) (onboarding wizard step 3 "??????"). Verbatim board explanation:

> Inside the Input there is "hire first engineer" and "create hiring plan". Unfortunately, Paperclip works poorly with the Russian language, and therefore I ask specifically these prompts, which are the task name, inside the Input the task text itself not to translate. That is, let it remain in English.
